sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of back pain for approximately four months, initially related to moving boxes, and describes a sharp pain that subsides with positioning. He reports tingling in his toes, which has resolved. He also mentions difficulty carrying heavy objects due to a sensation of instability. He reports taking ibuprofen and acetaminophen, with the ibuprofen providing slightly more relief but for a shorter duration. He denies stomach upset from the ibuprofen. He reports no bladder or bowel symptoms.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The physical exam reveals decreased flexion and extension of the lower back, a positive straight leg raise, and spasm of the paraspinal muscles. Vital signs are stable: blood pressure 117/76, heart rate 68, oxygen saturation 95% on room air. The lumbar spine x-ray showed no acute bony abnormality. Neurologic exam reveals equal strength bilaterally.
medical_concepts: decreased flexion of the lower back
decreased extension of the lower back

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient is diagnosed with an acute lumbar disc herniation, based on reported symptoms and physical exam findings.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am ordering a lumbar spine MRI to further evaluate the findings. I am referring the patient to physical therapy. I am prescribing meloxicam 15 mg daily for pain management. I will follow up with the patient to discuss potential epidural steroid injection if symptoms persist.

medical_concepts: decreased flexion of the lower back
decreased extension of the lower back
